popup.sidePanelTitle: Opens the full translator in the browser side panel (Chromium 114+)
popup.pageTranslateUnavailable: Page translation is not available on this page. Open a normal web page and try again.
popup.noProvider: No active provider configured. Open Settings to add one.
popup.alreadyActive: This page is already being translated
popup.sidePanelNotAvailable: Side panel is not available in this browser. Try recent Chrome or Edge (Chromium).
popup.cannotDetectWindow: Could not detect the browser window.

sidepanel.noProvider: No active provider configured. Open Settings to add one.

options.selectionPageDesc: Default languages for text selection and full-page translation. Popup and side panel use separate language preferences.
options.fallbackDesc: When the default provider fails, LinguaLens tries these providers in order. Leave empty to disable fallback.
options.noProviders: No providers configured. Add one above.
options.disableThinkingDesc: Skip model reasoning for faster translation
options.ollamaCorsWarning: Ollama requires CORS configuration for browser extensions. Start Ollama with:
options.promptTemplateDesc: Customize the system prompt for LLM-based providers. Available variables:
options.strategyQualityDesc: Translate larger blocks for better context and quality
options.strategySpeedDesc: Translate smaller chunks for faster progressive results
options.keyboardShortcutsDesc: Default shortcuts (if assigned by the browser): <kbd>Alt+T</kbd> translate selection (starts translation in the panel whenever the selection can be read — in the page or from the background), <kbd>Alt+Shift+T</kbd> translate page. Selecting text with the mouse already shows the floating trigger — the shortcut is mainly for keyboard-first use or when you want one-step translate without clicking the trigger. If a shortcut does nothing, open shortcut settings and bind LinguaLens to a free combination. Content scripts do not run on restricted pages (for example browser internal URLs or extension store pages).
options.welcomeDesc: Before translating, set up at least one provider below. Ollama and LM Studio work locally; cloud APIs need a base URL, model, and API key where required.
options.welcomeStep1: Enable a provider and check its URL and model
